reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome real download+run on WASM, cached auto-init re-test, light+dark, mobile+desktop, every sub-page driven) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser runs (headless Chromium 141, WASM, fresh profile → 'Download model (~110 MB)' → click → auto-run): the model embedded the 6 default sentences and rendered the 768-d cosine matrix in 1074 ms — battery pair 0.62, refund pair 0.80, cross-topic ≤ 0.24, unrelated garden line ≤ 0.11; PCA explains 64% variance. Pre-build standalone harness (same CDN transformers.js 3.7.5, q8): MODEL_READY 11.0 s, 4-sentence batch [4,768] in 749 ms, sim(eat food, eat bread)=0.6307 vs sim(eat food, carry baby)=-0.0260. 0 console errors, 0 failed requests on every driven page.
rubric.notes: Semantically correct ranking verified twice (page + standalone), never faked.

rubric.evidence: feature-extraction with mean pooling → [n, 768] (page readout dims=768; harness dims=[4,768]). Worker pools with normalize:false and L2-normalizes in JS (pre-norm ‖v‖ 2.745 shown in See-inside), so cosine = dot product on unit vectors. Manual average of the per-token vectors equals the pipeline's pooling:'mean' output exactly (harness: manual mean[0]=-0.15458 == pipeline -0.15458) — the pooling story shown to visitors is mathematically verified.
rubric.notes: Mean-pooling decomposition (per-token share sums to 100%) is exact, not illustrative.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact ONNX repo (Xenova/all-mpnet-base-v2, linked) AND the canonical source (sentence-transformers/all-mpnet-base-v2, linked), task, MPNet architecture (109M, masked+permuted pre-training), 768-d output, mean pooling + L2-normalize per the model card, NO prefixes (symmetric space — contrasted with arctic-embed/e5), 384-word-piece truncation, q8 quantisation, WASM backend, ~110 MB download, Apache-2.0. Inference runs in a module Web Worker (worker.js).
rubric.notes: Distinctness vs the built all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (same family, smaller) stated explicitly with a link.

rubric.evidence: Shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (no hand-rolled Load button). State-matrix driven on the real page: fresh profile → 'This model isn't on your device yet' + 'Download model (~110 MB)', controls disabled, NO auto-download; after click → ready + auto-run. Revisit with the SAME profile → 'Model ready — running locally on your device' + 'Clear cached model' + '110 MB cached' with NO download button, results in 7 s with zero clicks. Multi-model page: cached MPNet auto-inits while the absent reranker honestly shows its own Download (~23 MB).
rubric.notes: Cached-auto-init and fresh-visit states both captured in screenshots (mpnet-main-ready.png, mpnet-main-cached.png).

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live regions on every page report Embedding…/Done./failure text; the shared loader streams download progress and offers Retry on failure; errors land on the page (e.g. 'Embedding failed: …', 'Pipeline failed: …'), not only the console. Practical FAQ demo has an explicit honest no-answer state ('No FAQ clears cosine X — route to a human') verified in the driven run. WASM path — no WebGPU gate needed.
rubric.notes: Honest empty/no-match states exercised, not just success paths.

rubric.evidence: Every visible control driven in headless runs: main page (sentences textarea, Embed & compare, Reset, semantic-search input + Search + Enter key, inspect <select>); basics (A/B textareas, Compare, 3 example chips, odd-one-out list + button — fog/mist paraphrase 0.5582 'related', oven line flagged odd-one-out in 376 ms); practical (FAQ list, question input + chips, answer-threshold slider, tickets list, duplicate-threshold slider — 'my parcel never showed up' matched the shipping-trace FAQ, exactly the 2 planted duplicate pairs found at ≥0.60 in 608 ms); wild (guess input + Guess — real guess scored cosine 0.170 '🧊 cool' in 87 ms — New round, Give up & reveal, A/B/C algebra inputs + chips — cake−chocolate+lemon → 'A recipe for zesty lemon tart' ranked #1 in 626 ms); multi-model (docs textarea, query + chips, top-k slider — stage1 cosine 0.622/0.399 → stage2 logits 4.98/1.38 with ↑↓ movement badges, retrieve 970 ms + rerank 165 ms).
rubric.notes: Every rung's demo produced real, sensible output under automation.

rubric.evidence: Matrix, PCA, rankings, token chips, warmth meter and rerank logits are all computed from on-device inference (verified real above). The Hot & Cold deck uses Math.random only to PICK the round's secret; all scoring is the model's cosine (stated on the page). The multi-model page explicitly tells visitors the reranker can be confidently wrong out-of-domain (third chip) instead of overclaiming.
rubric.notes: Failure modes surfaced honestly as part of the demo's story.

rubric.evidence: All inputs/textareas/selects/sliders have associated <label>s; buttons are real <button>s with names; state announced via role=status aria-live; the PCA SVG has role=img + aria-label; token chips carry title tooltips with the raw numbers; Enter submits search/guess/query inputs; scoped overflow-wrap:anywhere keeps long ids from breaking layout. Light + dark reviewed from screenshots (design-system variables only — no raw hex for text/bg in the new pages beyond the shared palette).
rubric.notes: conformance run derives buttons-have-names/labels as pass (14✓).

rubric.evidence: scripts/responsive-check.mjs --slug all-mpnet-base-v2: desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean — 0px horizontal overflow, no clipped controls, clean console + network. Agent pass: mobile full-page screenshot reviewed (single column, matrix scrolls inside .matrix-scroll, readable text, ≥44px buttons); desktop light + dark screenshots reviewed. scrollWidth == innerWidth confirmed programmatically on the cached mobile run.
rubric.notes: Empty-looking use-case cards in beyond-viewport screenshots are the design system's content-visibility:auto render-skip, not a page bug (same as every built page).

rubric.evidence: All inference (embed, inspect, rerank) runs in module Web Workers; the main thread only does cheap maths over ≤ a dozen 768-d vectors (cosine/PCA/threshold filters). Measured: 6-sentence embed 1074 ms, single-guess embed 87 ms, mean-pooling inspect 134 ms, cross-encoder rerank of 4 pairs 165 ms — all in-worker on WASM. Sliders (thresholds, top-k, Matryoshka-style controls) recompute client-side over cached vectors only where no re-inference is needed; threshold sliders that re-run inference debounce through button/change events.
rubric.notes: 0 console errors on every driven page.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Offload / break up long main-thread tasks so the browser can paint and respond.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all inference (embed, per-token inspect, cross-encoder rerank) runs in module Web Workers (worker.js, rerank-worker.js); the main thread only ranks/filters a handful of cached 768-d vectors.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: queried 'long-running worker task progress streaming without blocking main thread INP'; 0 console errors + responsive UI in all driven runs

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep critical interaction work first; defer background work.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — user-triggered runs disable only their own button while in flight and re-enable in finally; nothing long-running ever executes on the interaction path itself.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: run()/ask()/guess()/go() handlers await worker promises with disabled-state management

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Audit INP contributors; avoid synchronous heavy loops on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the heaviest main-thread work is the n×n cosine matrix over ≤ ~12 unit vectors (microseconds); PCA power-iteration runs on the n×n Gram matrix, not the 768-d space.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: queried 'long-running worker task progress streaming'; mpnet.js pca2d uses the centered Gram trick (n ≪ d)

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid grids that wrap; wide content scrolls in its own container; long unbreakable tokens must wrap.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .grid-2 uses repeat(auto-fit,minmax(300px,1fr)); the similarity matrix scrolls inside .matrix-scroll (overflow-x:auto); scoped .inside-table overflow-wrap:anywhere wraps the long model ids.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: queried 'responsive two-column control panel mobile no horizontal overflow'; responsive-check measured 0px overflow both classes

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Accessible form controls with clear associated labels; Enter submits.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — every textarea/input/select/range has an associated label; Enter triggers search/guess/pipeline; buttons are real buttons with visible names.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: queried 'semantic similarity demo UI: textareas, ranked results list, progress and error states'; conformance derives labels/names as pass

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Semantic structure, landmarks, native elements over ARIA re-implementation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — header/section/footer landmarks, native <button>/<select>/<progress> (via shared loader), role=status live regions only where state changes.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: queried 'semantic similarity demo UI'; page markup mirrors the established shell

openQuestions: Should the See-inside token-share strip also offer a 'hide sub-word merges' toggle that aggregates ##-pieces into whole words for non-technical visitors?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a clustering rung (k-means over user-pasted feedback with theme labels from nearest-centroid sentences) — the third classic all-mpnet deployment besides search and dedup.
followUpGoals.rationale: kmeans() already ships in mpnet.js; a practical-2 page would demonstrate triage without any new model download.

followUpGoals.goal: Contrast page idea: same texts embedded by all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (built) vs all-mpnet-base-v2 side by side — quality-vs-size tradeoff made tangible with real numbers.
followUpGoals.rationale: Both models are cached after their pages are visited; a NEW slug comparing them would reuse existing workers.

summary: Built to the full bar: the canonical sentence-transformers workhorse running genuinely in-browser (q8 WASM, module workers), with an exact mean-pooling decomposition as the See-inside surface, four use-case rungs whose every demo was driven headlessly to real, semantically-correct output (paraphrase 0.56, planted duplicate pairs found, lemon-tart algebra #1, retrieve-then-rerank with honest failure chip), the shared auto-init loader verified across fresh/cached states, and 0 console errors on every page in light+dark, mobile+desktop.
